blurb: Faculty is a London-based applied-AI company with deep UK public-sector and government work (NHS, defence, central government 'decision intelligence'); a key node in the UK gov-AI delivery ecosystem.

publications.title: How we're building AI to help fix the English planning system
publications.summary: Faculty details applied AI work on the English planning system — directly relevant to the UK government's AI-for-planning agenda (parallels DeepMind's June planning-prototype announcement), and to how public-sector AI delivery actually lands.

publications.title: You don't have an AI problem. You have a decision problem.
publications.summary: Faculty's 'decision intelligence' framing — positioning argument about where AI value actually comes from in enterprise/government, useful as context on how a major UK gov-AI vendor pitches its work.

publications.title: Beyond manual red teaming: Scaling model safety with an Automated Red-teaming Emulation System
publications.summary: Faculty on automating red-teaming for model safety — an AI-assurance/evaluations capability, relevant given Faculty's safety work with UK bodies. Listing showed no inline date for this item; date left null.

news.title: Accenture to Acquire Faculty to Scale AI Capabilities
news.summary: Accenture to acquire Faculty — a material structural change for one of the UK government's most embedded AI vendors, with implications for who delivers (and owns) public-sector AI capability. Worth flagging for the conflict-of-interest / market-concentration lens.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via OpenClaw headless browser. faculty.ai/blog is a JS app — the article cards only render after a scroll-triggered lazy-load (initial DOM had just the section header), so this was missed by web_fetch this morning. Dates read from the inline date string on each card (e.g. '2026-06-16'); the red-teaming card had no inline date so date=null. Selected policy/public-sector-relevant items + the Accenture acquisition (high structural signal); omitted pure product launches (Frontier 3) and routine personnel posts. NB: the Accenture acquisition is a strong watch-item — a major UK gov-AI contractor changing ownership. ROLES (2026-06-27 ATS): Faculty is actively building a London AI-Safety team — a Technical Director of AI Safety plus Principal and Senior AI-Safety Research Scientists open simultaneously. A standing-up of dedicated AI-safety muscle ('building muscle' signal) at a major UK gov-AI vendor.
